message:
fix(linalg): change eigenvalue sort order to ascending (NumPy convention)

Fix eigvalsh_nxn() and eigh_nxn() to return eigenvalues in ascending
order instead of descending, matching NumPy's numpy.linalg.eigvalsh()
and numpy.linalg.eigh() conventions. NumPy guarantees eigenvalues are
"in ascending order, each repeated according to its multiplicity"
(numpy docs). The prior descending sort caused downstream code relying
on NumPy's ordering contract to produce incorrect results, particularly
in positive-definiteness checks and spectral decompositions where the
smallest eigenvalue is expected at index 0.

The eigenvector permutation in eigh_nxn() is updated correspondingly
so eigenvectors remain paired with their eigenvalues after reordering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
